title: MIDI Control Keymap
description: A keymap that maps MIDI notes to Guild Wars 2 keyboard keys for the Blish HUD MIDI Control module.

$schema.description: Reference to this JSON Schema for editor validation and autocomplete.

id.description: Unique identifier for this keymap. Used for persistence and collision detection. Must be unique across all keymaps (built-in and custom).

name.description: Human-readable name displayed in the settings dropdown.

autoOctaveSwap.description: When true, the module automatically tracks the current in-game octave and sends octave-shift keys (9/0) when a note is outside the current range.

octaveDownKey.description: The keyboard key that shifts the in-game instrument down one octave. Only used when autoOctaveSwap is true.

octaveUpKey.description: The keyboard key that shifts the in-game instrument up one octave. Only used when autoOctaveSwap is true.

notes.description: Mapping from MIDI note names to note definitions. Each key is a MIDI note name (e.g. C4, D#4, Bb5).

^[A-G]#?\\d+$.description: Definition of how a single MIDI note maps to a keyboard key.

key.description: The keyboard key to send when this MIDI note is received. Required unless forceInternalOctave is set.

octave.description: The relative octave (0-5) this note belongs to. Used by auto-octave-swap logic. Omit for special/manual keys (e.g. octave shift buttons).

altOctave.description: An alternative relative octave where this note can be played without shifting octaves, using a different key.

altOctaveKey.description: The keyboard key to use when playing this note on the altOctave. Only meaningful when altOctave is also set.

forceInternalOctave.description: Updates the module's internal octave tracker without sending a keypress. Used for notes that change the active octave implicitly (e.g. black keys on The Minstrel).
